habitat: Woodland; plantations; open forest; forest edges; and moist areas near water
story: Meet the Long Crested Eagle (Lophaetus occipitalis), a distinctive bird from the Accipitridae family. Preferring habitats such as Woodland; plantations; open forest; forest edges; and moist areas near water, this species has adapted to thrive under specific environmental conditions. With an IUCN status of Least Concern (LC), its population trends are closely watched by scientists and enthusiasts. The Long Crested Eagle serves as an indicator species for the health of its environment. Whether you are a seasoned ornithologist or a curious nature lover, learning about the Long Crested Eagle enriches our understanding of nature.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
